tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement over the next ~2 years, with the median price of $66,996 essentially flat versus current spot of $67,006. The interquartile range of $66,469 to $67,523 (25th-75th percentile) suggests markets are pricing in a tight range of just ~1.6% either direction, with the 10th-90th percentile bounds spanning $66,153 to $67,840 (approximately ±1.3%).

tenors.narrative: With minimal market activity, prediction markets suggest modest WTI upside to a median of $129 (+16%) by end-April 2026, though the wide 10th-90th percentile range ($75-$159) reflects substantial uncertainty. The distribution skews toward higher prices, with the 75th percentile at $142, indicating market participants see more tail risk to the upside than downside.

Despite $642k in volume across 14 markets, consensus points to a sharp WTI decline to $82 (-26%) by June 2026, with the median significantly below the 25th percentile ($71), suggesting a bimodal distribution. The extreme 90th percentile at $200 signals meaningful tail risk, but the 75th percentile at $150 indicates the market is roughly split between a deflationary scenario and substantially elevated prices.

tenors.narrative: Markets expect gold to decline modestly by mid-2026, with the median price of $4,610 implying a -1.2% pullback from current spot. The 25th-75th percentile range ($3,971-$5,502) reflects substantial uncertainty, with tail risks extending to $3,611 (downside) and $5,969 (upside), suggesting markets are pricing in significant macroeconomic dispersion scenarios over the next 18 months.
